candidates.studentStem: A:T+0,7/20 före, insekter36->29, blomning42->35. B:insekter36->29 före T+0,7/20, blomning42->35. C:T+0,7/2 före, insekter36->29, blomning42->35. D:varm dag24, medel stabilt, insekter36->29. Vilken orsak är rimligast?
candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has T plus 0.7 over 20 years before insect 36 to 29 and flowering 42 to 35. B reverses order, C uses two years and D has only a warm day with stable average. Correct A is the strongest cause. (b) Distractor types: B is Type II reversed-cause shortcut, C is Type III short-period shortcut, D is Type I weather-event shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: time-order reasoning, climate-versus-weather distinction, insect data reading and flowering-timing inference. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests cause evidence for warming effects. (e) Knowledge link map: long trend -> insects and flowering. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: neutral option labels and multiple shared cues prevent one-fact solving.

candidates.construction_note_en: Sections: (a) Numeric verification: A has T plus 0.7 over 20 years, insects 36 to 29, feeding 45 to 45 and foraging 63 to 42. B changes feeding and keeps foraging high, C lacks temperature and D uses two years. Correct A is the coherent relationship. (b) Distractor types: B is Type II mismatched-response shortcut, C is Type I missing-driver shortcut, D is Type III short-window shortcut. (c) Cognitive dimensions: food-chain reasoning, control-variable reading, bird-response interpretation and distractor elimination. (d) Specific knowledge vs general ability: the item tests a warming-insect-bird relation. (e) Knowledge link map: warming -> insects -> controlled feeding -> foraging. (f) Text audit and shortcut check: labels are neutral and no stem keyword appears in the correct option.
